name: Plinth base
description: Raised stone base wrapping the entire building perimeter, one or two blocks tall, sitting at y = 0 directly beneath the wall plane. Built from a denser, heavier material than the wall above — smooth stone, polished andesite or rough cobblestone against timber, plaster or sandstone walls — so the visual weight grades from solid below to lighter above. Functionally it lifts the wall course off wet ground, breaks capillary rise of damp, and protects the lower fabric from splash and frost; symbolically it marks the threshold between earth and inhabited volume, anchoring the building to its site. The plinth may sit flush with the wall or project outward by one block, casting a continuous shadow line that articulates the base. Universal classical detail across medieval, renaissance and mediterranean traditions.
